what_it_does: A self-contained React (no build step, global-window) library that ships a floating, draggable 'Tweaks' editing panel plus a set of form-control components (slider, toggle, segmented radio, select, text, number-scrubber, curated color/palette picker, button) for live-editing prototype parameters; it also owns the postMessage host protocol that activates/deactivates edit mode and persists tweak values back to disk.
observations: Top line marks it @ds-adherence-ignore as an 'omelette starter scaffold' that intentionally uses raw hex/px
Large inline CSS string (__TWEAKS_STYLE) styles all controls; macOS-flavoured glassmorphism (backdrop-filter blur/saturate, translucent backgrounds)
useTweaks hook is the single source of truth for values; setTweak accepts either (key,value) or an edits object
setTweak posts __edit_mode_set_keys to window.parent AND dispatches a same-window 'tweakchange' CustomEvent so in-page peers can react
TweakRadio auto-falls-back to TweakSelect past width thresholds (2 options ~16 chars, 3 ~10) with detailed comment explaining the px math
TweakColor accepts a single hex or an array palette; renders hero color + stacked supporting swatches; computes checkmark contrast via relative-luminance (__twkIsLight)
TweakNumber label is a drag-to-scrub control (ew-resize) with decimal snapping from step
Panel is draggable, clamps to viewport via ResizeObserver (with resize-listener fallback), reads --dc-inv-zoom for inverse-zoom scaling
All components Object.assign'd onto window for use in Babel-in-browser HTML pages
EDITMODE-BEGIN/EDITMODE-END markers in the usage example delimit the JSON block the host rewrites on disk
standouts: The bidirectional host protocol (iframe<->host) with explicit ordering: registers the message listener BEFORE announcing __edit_mode_available to avoid a race where activate lands before the handler exists
Persistence model where setTweak rewrites a marked JSON block on disk via the host rather than localStorage
Width-fit heuristic that picks segmented control vs dropdown from measured character budgets
conventions: Global-window component registration (no imports/exports) — Babel-standalone prototype pattern
Type-preserving option handling: select/radio map string DOM values back to original number/boolean option values
Components documented inline in a /* BEGIN USAGE */ block with a runnable example
Explicitly opts out of the design-system token discipline (raw hex/px by design, flagged at top)
concerns: Uses postMessage targetOrigin '*' for parent messages (no origin restriction)
Reads window.parent.postMessage unconditionally; in a top-level (non-iframe) context parent === self, so the protocol still fires
notes: If briefing someone: this is the reusable edit-mode chrome + control kit for the 'omelette' prototype system — drop it in, wrap state in useTweaks, and a host can flip edit mode and write changes back to the file.
questions: What is the 'host' that rewrites the EDITMODE block on disk, and where does it live?
What is the 'omelette' system this scaffold belongs to?
What consumes the same-window 'tweakchange' event besides the mentioned 'deck-stage rail thumbnails'?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the header describes a reusable Tweaks shell + control helpers owning the host protocol, and the code implements exactly that set of exports and protocol.
apparent_themes: control kit as a floor not a ceiling
intention_signals: Aims to remove boilerplate from every prototype: one place owns the edit-mode protocol and a consistent set of polished controls, so prototype authors only declare a defaults JSON block and bind controls, and edits flow back to source.
novelty: The disk-write-back persistence via host postMessage + EDITMODE JSON markers, and the drag-scrub number label, are unusual touches for a prototype tweak panel.
contribution: Contributes a reusable edit-mode panel + a full set of styled live-edit form controls and the host/iframe protocol for activating edit mode and persisting tweaks back to source files.
summary_for_embedding: A global-window React Tweaks panel library with a full set of live-edit form controls and a postMessage host protocol that persists tweak values back to disk for in-browser prototypes.
intention_for_embedding: To give every prototype a consistent, ready-made live-editing panel and controls, with edits single-sourced and written back to the source file via a host protocol.
